evals.name: question-answering
evals.prompt: Why did we decide to use jq for JSON escaping in the session-start hook instead of doing it in bash?
evals.expected_output: Searches docs/ for jq/session-start/hook keywords. Reads shell-hook-scripts.md and follows wikilinks (codeless-architecture-decision, autology-internals). Synthesizes answer citing specific docs: jq handles all control characters and unicode correctly; bash parameter substitution failed on glob characters. Answer is grounded in docs, not just general knowledge.

assertions.description: Grep or search of docs/ is performed before answering — not answered from memory
At least one wikilinked node is followed (1-hop) for additional context
Answer mentions unicode/control character handling or bash escaping failure as the reason for jq

evals.name: overview-operation
evals.expected_output: Reads all docs/*.md, extracts frontmatter and wikilinks. Reports: total node count, link count, component count, top 5 hub nodes (most connections), orphan nodes (no links). Format matches the overview output spec.

assertions.description: All docs/*.md files are globbed and read
Top hub nodes (most wikilink connections) are identified and listed
Total node count and link count are reported as numbers

evals.name: neighborhood-traversal
evals.expected_output: Reads docs/autology-internals.md. Finds all nodes that link to or from it (1-hop). Then finds their connections (2-hop BFS). Reports node title, type, tags, and connections at each hop. Does not stop at 1-hop.

assertions.description: Nodes directly linked to/from autology-internals are identified
2-hop neighbors (neighbors of neighbors) are also found and reported
Each node in the output includes its type and tags
